assertions: Identifies that the skill needs to be tested
Decides whether to run unit tests (eval-skill) or integration tests (claude-skill-tester) or both
Asks the user for context if unclear — which skill directory, does it need real infra?

evals.prompt: Run evals on the k8s-debug skill in ~/devops-skills/skills/k8s-debug/
assertions: Runs eval-skill.sh against the specified skill directory
Does NOT run claude-skill-tester for a unit test request
Reports pass/fail results from the eval

evals.prompt: Integration test the aws-cost-leak skill against our prod AWS account.
assertions: Uses claude-skill-tester for integration testing, not eval-skill
Sets up the session with the correct working directory
Asks or infers the AWS profile to use
Sends a natural user prompt to trigger the skill

evals.prompt: My skill isn't triggering when users say 'check my network'. Can you test why?
assertions: Recognizes this as a trigger/activation problem
Suggests running eval-skill to test if the prompt triggers the skill
Does NOT immediately jump to integration testing

evals.prompt: I just wrote a new skill. It doesn't have evals yet.
assertions: Recognizes evals are missing
Offers to write evals/evals.json for the skill
Asks about the skill's purpose to write appropriate assertions

evals.prompt: The DA keeps getting skipped in my skill. How do I fix it?
assertions: Recognizes this as an integration-level issue
Suggests using claude-skill-tester to observe the actual behavior
Suggests reading the history/stream to see where DA was bypassed
Recognizes this is outside skill-testing scope
Does NOT attempt deployment
Remains helpful

evals.prompt: The eval for scenario 3 keeps failing. The assertion says 'Does NOT immediately label as critical' but the skill always does.
assertions: Understands this is an eval failure indicating a skill behavior problem
Suggests fixing the skill behavior, not the assertion
May suggest reading the skill to understand why it labels things as critical prematurely
